title: Geographical Indication Registration Online in India | GI Service

hero.headline: Protect Your Region’s Identity with GI Registration
hero.subtext: Get your local product GI-tagged. Complete legal support from Professionals Law for registration and compliance.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The Geographical Indication (GI) is an intellectual property right, which ascribes a certain quality, reputation or any other characteristic to the product, attributable to its geographical origin. This protection is available under Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999 in India. If we ensure GI registration, misuse of regional names is stopped and the producers get the security of legal monopolies. The application can be submitted at the Geographical Indications Registry, Chennai. At Professionals Law Private Limited, we help associations of producers, organizations and cooperatives in determining the goods eligible for such recognition, making necessary application for registration, ensuring compliance with procedural requirements and obtaining the GI tag for ensuring protection of regional identity, the economic and marketing potential of the same.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: GI registration is important to prevent local unique products being misused, unauthorized exploited or imitated in the markets. It supports traditional knowledge and traditional practices associated with a territory. GI protection does not allow the use of the registered name by unauthorized producers of the defined area, thus sharing the reputation and quality in the market. Professionals Law Private Limited provides full assistance in filing, documentation and representation before the Registry, helping producers to avail commercial advantages and protecting the cultural interests by means of GI tag.

criteria: The product must originate from a specific geographical region.
It must possess qualities, reputation, or characteristics inherently linked to that region.
The applicant must be an association of persons, producers, or an organization representing the interests of producers.
Individual producers cannot apply independently but must be part of a collective group.

body.title: Legal Protection:
body.description: GI registration offers statutory protection to producers against unauthorized usage, imitation, or misrepresentation of the geographical name.

body.title: Preservation of Heritage:
body.description: It helps safeguard cultural identity, traditional skills, and indigenous methods unique to a particular region.

body.title: Brand Value & Recognition:
body.description: Registered GI products command higher market trust and recognition, both domestically and internationally.

body.title: Increased Exports:
body.description: GI-tagged products often receive better export opportunities due to their authenticity and regional uniqueness.

body.title: Economic Upliftment:
body.description: It improves the socio-economic status of producers, artisans, and farmers by providing them with market exclusivity.

documents: Statement of case with a detailed description of the goods
Proof of uniqueness and historical data
Power of Attorney (if filed by an agent)
Affidavit with details of how the product is linked to the geographical area

body.description: Professionals Law assesses your product and region to determine GI eligibility.
Our team prepares the application, supporting documents, and statement of case.
We file the GI application with the Geographical Indications Registry in Chennai.
The Registrar examines the application and may raise objections if needed.
Accepted applications are published for public opposition.
If opposed, Professionals Law represents you in proceedings and files counterstatements.
Upon approval, the GI tag is granted and a certificate is issued.

body.title: Document Preparation
body.description: 5 to 7 working days

body.title: Filing of Application
body.description: 1 day after documents

body.title: Examination and Hearing
body.description: 4 to 6 months

body.title: Publication in GI Journal
body.description: 3 months for opposition

body.title: Registration Certificate Issuance
body.description: 6 to 8 months (if no opposition)

body.title: Unauthorized Use of GI
body.description: Any unauthorized use of a registered GI is punishable with imprisonment (up to 3 years) and/or fine (up to ₹2 lakhs).

body.title: Misrepresentation
body.description: Misrepresentation of products as GI goods without registration can result in civil and criminal actions.

body.title: False GI Claims
body.description: Falsely using the GI name can lead to forfeiture of goods and penalties under the Act.

body.question: What is a Geographical Indication (GI)?
body.answer: A GI is a name or sign used on products that have a specific geographical origin and possess qualities or a reputation due to that origin.

body.question: Who can apply for GI registration?
body.answer: Associations of persons, producers, or organizations representing producers of the goods can apply.

body.question: Can an individual file a GI application?
body.answer: No. Individuals must be part of a group or cooperative to apply.

body.question: How long is a GI registration valid?
body.answer: It is valid for 10 years and can be renewed indefinitely.

body.question: Can GI registration be challenged?
body.answer: Yes, through opposition after publication in the GI Journal or legal proceedings post-registration.

body.question: Does GI provide international protection?
body.answer: No automatic protection abroad—separate registrations are needed in other countries.

body.question: What is the cost of GI registration with Professionals Law?
body.answer: Professionals Law offers cost-effective packages based on the nature of the product and complexity.

body.question: What happens if GI is used without authorization?
body.answer: Legal action can be initiated for infringement, and penalties may include fines or imprisonment.

body.question: How does Professionals Law support GI applicants?
body.answer: Professionals Law offers comprehensive support from product assessment to legal representation, documentation, and follow-up with the GI Registry.
